title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The government has created a policy to address AI and public standards, aiming to maintain trust and confidence in AI systems, after a report highlighted the need for improvement. This policy is a response to a 2020 report on Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards, with the goal of implementing changes and setting standards for AI use.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This document assesses the impact of using copyrighted works in the development of artificial intelligence systems. It provides a report on the copyright-related issues arising from AI system development.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: The Department for Education outlines its stance on using generative artificial intelligence in education.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: The DfT Transport artificial intelligence action plan aims to help the transport sector use AI to create benefits, while minimising any associated risks. It involves collaboration between the DfT and the sector to ensure safe and effective use of AI in transport.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This progress report examines the use of copyrighted materials in AI system development, as required by law. It presents updates on the use of copyrighted works in creating AI systems, likely focusing on compliance and implications.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: The UK government is responding to recommendations on how to regulate AI that is used as a medical device, to ensure public safety and effective use. The government will work on implementing new guidelines or laws based on the RHC's suggestions.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: This document outlines Ofqual's approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence (AI) in qualifications. It responds to a government request, indicating it's a policy framework for Ofqual's actions on AI in the qualifications sector.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government has provided a response to the House of Lords Select Committee's report on artificial intelligence, titled 'AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency'. The response addresses concerns and recommendations from the committee report, outlining the government's actions and plans for regulating and improving AI in the UK.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: This document outlines the terms and frequently asked questions for a committee's review of artificial intelligence and public standards. The review aims to evaluate how AI aligns with societal expectations and public values.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The Cabinet Office's Centre for Strategic Policy and Legislation has submitted evidence to an inquiry on how to govern artificial intelligence in the UK. Their submission likely provides recommendations on regulations and oversight for AI systems.
